title: Track and verify external reuse events
context: The project's M3 success metric requires at least three verifiable external reuse events — a tutorial cited in a paper, forked with activity, linked from official docs, or referenced in an upstream issue. Self-reported reuse does not count; only externally verifiable evidence (URL, DOI, commit) qualifies. Depends on contributions from M1 and M2 (contrib-011, contrib-013, contrib-015) having been delivered and given time to accumulate reuse signals.
objective: Identify, document, and commit at least three externally verifiable reuse events for delivered contributions, with evidence linking to externally accessible sources (citation URL, fork URL, docs link, issue URL).
acceptanceCriteria: At least three verifiable external reuse events are recorded: e.g., a tutorial cited in a publication (DOI/URL), a fork with activity referencing our contribution, our docs linked from the tool's official documentation, or an upstream issue referencing our tutorial.
Each event links to externally verifiable evidence — no self-report; the evidence URL/DOI/commit must be independently accessible.
Reuse events are committed as a document in the outcome ledger.
Steward reviews and confirms each event meets the 'externally verifiable' bar.
The document feeds into the M3 Definition of Done metric.
resources: PLAN.md — Success metrics (Verifiable external reuse row)
Outcome artifacts from contrib-011, contrib-013, contrib-015
output: A committed reuse-events record with at least three externally verifiable evidence items (URL/DOI/commit), fulfilling the M3 reuse metric and demonstrating downstream beneficiary impact.
